rationale: The cited paper is used in a specific modeling step, not just as general background. In the "Handling Very Low-Cost Media" section, Danaher cites Chae et al. (2019) as evidence that high ad volume can create annoyance/weariness and reduce effectiveness, then introduces a negative quadratic email term to penalize excessive exposures. This citation therefore helps justify a concrete refinement to the utility and optimization setup, even though the cited paper's full method is not reused.
relation_description: Danaher applies Chae et al.'s weariness insight to motivate and parameterize a diminishing/negative response at high email volume. This leads to adding a quadratic penalty term for email in the microtargeting model so optimal allocations do not over-send low-cost emails.
